_note: pocket.bundle.v1 SIGNED known-answer vector (warden/bundle-kav-fix, FIX2). Proves the Phase-A demo gateway key signs a bundle whose ed25519 signature the phone verifies under the PINNED public key. The keypair is a REAL random ed25519 pair generated ONCE: only the PUBLIC key + this signature are committed; the private key was ephemeral and is NOT committed or derivable from anything here. Relay's Node gateway (services/pocket-gateway) reproduces `canonicalBytesUtf8` byte-for-byte and signs REAL bundles with the real gateway key; the phone pins the real public key the same way. This fixture is wired as a PocketContracts test resource and loaded by ContractsCrossModuleTests (no duplicated literals).

demoKey.note: Real random ed25519 public key. The matching private key is NOT committed and NOT derivable from any committed value; a forged bundle cannot be signed under this key.

tamperVectors._note: Any mutation to a signed field changes canonicalBytesUtf8, so the committed signature no longer verifies. Semantic mutations are ALSO rejected by isSemanticallyValid(); an untrusted signingKeyId is rejected before crypto. A wrong-key signature (throwaway keypair) does not verify under the pinned public key.
examples: evidence order swapped                            (canonical changes: signature fails)
per-agent ev1 with a FOREIGN session             (semantic reject: per-agent evidence must match top-level)

verifyRecipe: pub = base64urlDecode(demoKey.publicKeyBase64url); ok = ed25519.verify(canonicalBytesUtf8 (utf8), base64urlDecode(kav.signatureBase64url), pub)  ->  true. (No private key is needed to verify; it is not committed.)
